customer_anxiety_profile: Steve is not anxious — he is experienced and self-directed. His risk profile is no-show due to spam filtering and loose time anchor, not cold feet. He may deprioritize the call if it does not come through clearly identified.
unresolved_concerns: Ion's number registers as potential spam on Steve's phone — no workaround was established
No specific Monday time was locked — 'anytime Monday' is the only commitment
Steve has not yet sent the utility bill — the appointment may proceed without it
sensitive_topics_raised: Steve has already claimed his own solar tax credits from his prior system and stated there is 'none anymore' — he is not expecting ITC benefits on a new install. The closer should not lead with tax credit framing; Steve will push back. Note: Steve's statement reflects his personal situation (credits already claimed), not a categorical policy change — the closer should be aware of Steve's framing without assuming it applies universally.
Steve's prior solar system was removed and not replaced due to age and cost — he has a specific negative data point ($2,020 to reinstall old panels) that shapes his cost sensitivity
exact_promises_rep_made: A specialist will call Steve on Monday (no specific time confirmed)
Randy will send an email to steve@sudser.com for bill submission
The appointment will be by phone — no in-person visit
closer_should_know: Steve is a prior solar owner with a brand-new roof, known utility (BGE), self-reported ~11,000 kWh/year usage, and a stated preference for leasing with a fixed-rate or escalator structure — he described the exact comparison he wants to see (25-year cost of BGE at 4% annual increase vs. lease option A vs. lease option B). He will not need education on solar basics and will be impatient if the specialist treats him like a first-time buyer. The spam-filter issue is the primary no-show risk: the closer should call from a clearly identified number or have Randy send Steve a heads-up text before the Monday call.
